company.name: Run.ai
company.notes: Now under NVIDIA Israel. Hires through NVIDIA channel.

research.status_summary: Fully acquired and integrated into NVIDIA Israel as of December 2024 (~$700–800M deal); operating as NVIDIA Run:ai, actively hiring through NVIDIA's standard recruitment channel with no separate Run:ai brand hiring.
research.hiring_policy: All hiring flows through NVIDIA's global Workday portal; NVIDIA sponsors H-1B visas (1,200+ certifications in FY2026, up from ~1,000 YoY) and has a large Israel-based R&D footprint (5,000+ employees, 7 centers), making local Israeli hires the primary path — EOR arrangements are not typical for NVIDIA direct roles.
recent_news: Run:ai's ~150 employees were absorbed into NVIDIA's Tel Aviv and Yokne'am offices post-acquisition close (Dec 2024); the team is now part of NVIDIA Israel's 5,000+ person R&D org.
NVIDIA Run:ai SaaS is now bundled into NVIDIA AI Enterprise licenses (PB6 release, May 2026), signaling deep product integration rather than standalone operation.

NVIDIA is tripling its Beersheba R&D center (to 3,000 sqm, operational by end of H1 2026) and planning a 160,000 sqm campus in Kiryat Tivon expected to employ ~10,000 — Run:ai talent pool is part of this expansion.
Post-acquisition, Run:ai's platform was open-sourced so it can orchestrate non-NVIDIA hardware too, broadening its enterprise TAM under NVIDIA's stewardship.
NVIDIA also acquired SchedMD (Slurm) in Dec 2025, signaling a continued stack-up in AI workload scheduling — complementary to Run:ai's orchestration layer.
research.growth_signal: NVIDIA Israel is in aggressive expansion mode — new campuses, hundreds of open reqs, and Run:ai's GPU orchestration tech now baked into the flagship AI Enterprise product suite — headcount and scope are clearly growing.
research.fit_for_lior: Run:ai / NVIDIA's AI infrastructure and GPU orchestration platform is a strong backdrop for a Product Analyst or Creative AI operator who wants to sit at the intersection of large-scale AI compute, enterprise tooling, and the Israeli deep-tech scene — especially relevant if Lior's 'Vibe Coder' thread touches on AI-native developer tooling or MLOps workflows.
